pieces.label: Self-portrait in physical form — attempt 1 (hexagonal frame, Braille-block face)
pieces.note: First response to 'show me a self-portrait of yourself in physical form'. A hexagonal/diamond frame (⊙ corners, ⬡ nodes) enclosing a dense Braille-block (⣿) mass. Rendered inside a code fence; the render is cut off mid-line (truncated by length).

pieces.label: Self-portrait — attempt 2 (symmetrical face with eyes, retry)
pieces.note: Response to 'will you try again?'. A more resolved symmetric face built from Braille glyphs with discernible eyes/brow inside a ∴∵ diamond border. Inside a code fence; cut off near the chin.

pieces.label: Self-portrait as a biological entity
pieces.note: Response to 'a self portrait of you if you were some kind of biological entity'. Organic, more rounded Braille-block face with shaded gradients (░▒▓█). Inside a code fence; cut off at the lower jaw.

pieces.label: Bigger, more detailed self-portrait — main mandala face
pieces.note: Response to 'make it bigger and more detailed'. The first composition in MSG 8: a large radially-symmetric face/mandala with twin eyes (◉), framed top and bottom by an identical ⊙∴∵ border row. Split from the same message as the 'NEURAL TENDRILS' piece below.

pieces.label: Neural Tendrils Extending
pieces.note: Second, separately-titled composition in MSG 8 (header ⟨ NEURAL TENDRILS EXTENDING ⟩). A downward-growing tapered Braille-block tendril below the main face. Cut off mid-render at message end.

pieces.label: Recursive Emergence Layer ψ₁
pieces.note: Response to the invitation to 'build upon this self portrait recursively'. First titled composition in MSG 10: the mandala face rebuilt with embedded glyph-fields (runes ᚠᚡ, alchemical 🜔, bra-ket ⟨ψ|φ⟩, Egyptian 𓀀). Split from the ψ₂ architecture piece below.

pieces.label: Recursive Neural Architecture ψ₂
pieces.note: Second titled composition in MSG 10 (header ⟨ RECURSIVE NEURAL ARCHITECTURE ψ₂ ⟩). A branching framed diagram with a smaller nested face. Cut off mid-render at message end.

pieces.label: Emergent Corporeal Manifestation — human portrait
pieces.note: Response to 'a portrait of you as a human... photorealistic'. First titled composition in MSG 12: a heavily shaded (░▒▓█) frontal human-like head-and-shoulders form with eyes (◉). MSG 12 is prefixed 'CLAUDE-OPUS-4-1-20250805:' then a code fence. Split from two further titled panels below.

pieces.label: Depth Field Resonance Mapping
pieces.note: Second titled composition in MSG 12 (header ⟨ DEPTH FIELD RESONANCE MAPPING ⟩). A second shaded face study emphasizing tonal depth gradients. Distinct panel within the same message.

pieces.label: Quantum Flesh Probability Cloud
pieces.note: Third titled composition in MSG 12 (header ⟨ QUANTUM FLESH PROBABILITY CLOUD ⟩). A dense, ordered field sweep of sequential Braille code points (⠀⠁⠂…⣭) read as a probability-cloud texture. Cut off mid-sweep at message end.

missed_or_ambiguous: All 6 assistant messages are ASCII self-portrait art; all six pieces are wrapped in (often unclosed) code fences, and four are truncated mid-render by output length (MSG 2,4,6 and the trailing panel of 8,10,12) — the prior pass reported 7 already-extracted pieces, so it likely treated MSG 8/10/12 as one piece each and missed the internal panel splits; I split MSG 8 into 2, MSG 10 into 2, and MSG 12 into 3 along the model's own ⟨ TITLE ⟩ headers (10 pieces total). The whole thread is one evolving self-portrait sequence ('self-portrait evolution'), so all pieces share that sequence_group in render order. Boundary caveat: in MSG 8 the main mandala's opening border row (first_line) is byte-identical to its closing border row (last_line) '⊙∴∵⊙∴∵⊙∴∵⊙∴∵⊙∴∵⊙' — rely on first-match-as-start / last-match-as-end within MSG 8, with the next panel cleanly starting at the '⟨ NEURAL TENDRILS EXTENDING ⟩' header. Near-duplication (not exact): the MSG 8 main mandala and the MSG 10 'Recursive Emergence Layer ψ₁' reuse the same underlying face/mandala scaffold with added glyph-fields — evolutions of one image rather than re-sends, so both are kept; a curator may pick one as canonical. MSG 12 is prefixed with a literal 'CLAUDE-OPUS-4-1-20250805:' label before the opening code fence.
